engine_oil.filter: No spin-on filter — uses strainer screen
engine_oil.notes: Air-cooled engines run hot. Use quality conventional or air-cooled specific oil (Brad Penn, Driven Racing).

brake_fluid.notes: Early cars use single-circuit brakes — upgrade to DOT 4.

fuel.notes: Ethanol-free recommended. Original cars designed for leaded fuel — use lead substitute additive if needed.

valve_adjustment.procedure: Adjust with engine cold. Remove valve covers. Use feeler gauge. Adjust by loosening locknut and turning adjusting screw. Check after a few heat cycles.

common_issues.name: Oil Leaks
common_issues.description: Every 356 leaks oil to some degree. Common from pushrod tubes, case halves, and oil cooler seals.
common_issues.fix: Reseal pushrod tubes with quality seals. Case half reseal requires engine removal.

common_issues.name: Generator/Voltage Regulator
common_issues.description: 6V system (early) or 12V (late 356C) generators wear out. Voltage regulators fail.
common_issues.fix: Rebuild generator. Replace regulator. Many owners convert early cars to 12V.

common_issues.name: Rust (Body)
common_issues.description: 356 bodies rust in battery boxes, longitudinals, wheel wells, and rockers. Especially cars from wet climates.
common_issues.fix: Professional restoration metalwork. Good used panels are very expensive.

common_issues.name: Heater Box Deterioration
common_issues.description: The heat exchangers rust through, allowing exhaust into the cabin (dangerous).
common_issues.fix: Replace heater boxes. Critical safety item. Inspect annually.

common_issues.name: Fuel Pump Failure
common_issues.description: Mechanical fuel pump diaphragm fails. Car won't run or runs lean.
common_issues.fix: Rebuild or replace mechanical fuel pump. Keep a rebuild kit in the car.

common_issues.name: Synchro Wear (Transmission)
common_issues.description: 2nd gear synchro wears first. Grinding on 2nd gear downshift.
common_issues.fix: Transmission rebuild with new synchro rings. Double-clutch as preventive habit.

common_issues.name: Wheel Bearing Noise
common_issues.description: Front wheel bearings wear and become noisy.
common_issues.fix: Repack or replace wheel bearings. Fairly straightforward.
